meaning: n.煎煮,煮出的汁,煎熬的药
examples: The method can provide a reference for the material base research and quality control of the decoction of Rhizoma Polygonati.
The objective is to establish industrialization production technology parameter for decoction pieces shuangfei of Platycodon grandiflorum.
Another morning cup — boiled coffee — is a common example of a decoction.
The transition is a keen one, I assure you, from a schoolmaster to a sailor, and requires a strong decoction of Seneca and the Stoics to enable you to grin and bear it.
